data.title: Transport Manager Essential Skills Course: Reducing Human Error Through Process Review (Online Event: Oct 23, 2025) - ResearchAndMarkets.com.
data.description: A training course titled "Understanding and Managing Human Error - Essential Skills for the Transport Manager Training Course (Oct 23, 2025)" has been recently included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. This specialized one-day event aims to assist participants in recognizing various methods to evaluate and enhance work procedures to minimize the occurrence of human errors going forward. The course is tailored to address the unique scenarios and hazards related to the transport industry. Scheduled for October 23, 2025, the training course focuses on enhancing the skills of transport managers in handling human errors effectively.

data.title: Vow ASA secures EUR 11.3 million order for cruise newbuild; deliveries starting from mid-June 2026.
data.description: In Oslo on September 26, 2025, Vow ASA (stock symbol OSE: VOW) and its subsidiary Scanship have secured a substantial purchase order from a prominent European shipyard valued at EUR 11.3 million. The delivery of the order is scheduled to commence in mid-June 2026 and continue through 2027.

data.title: Rachel Platten headlines Susan G. Komen® Party in Pink Gala on 10th anniversary of "Fight Song." Event in NYC on Oct 8.
data.description: A Grammy-winning singer-songwriter, Rachel Platten, is set to perform at the Susan G. Komen® Party in Pink gala in New York City on October 8. This event, which marks the start of National Breast Cancer Awareness Month, will also celebrate the tenth anniversary of Platten's empowering anthem, “Fight Song.” Platten will use her voice to support the millions impacted by breast cancer and those fighting against it. Kathy Sutherland expressed excitement about having Rachel join the event.

data.title: Tech earnings and AI boost propel U.S. stocks to record highs. Fed rate cuts expected due to soft labor data and stable inflation. Market yielding and volatility ease.
data.description: Record-high levels were reached in U.S. equities due to positive tech earnings and optimism surrounding AI. Additionally, soft labor data and consistent inflation rates are increasing expectations of rate cuts by the Fed, leading to lower yields and reduced volatility in the market.

data.title: New OpenAI data reveals Claude and GPT-5 approaching human skill levels, with models improving by 300% in 15 months. Will AI replace jobs soon?
data.description: A recent study by OpenAI revealed that the models Claude and GPT-5 have reached a significant milestone by successfully matching the performance of human experts in real work tasks. What is even more astonishing is that these models have shown a remarkable improvement of 300% over a period of just 15 months.

data.title: Accenture's Q4 2025 results beat expectations, with earnings of $3.03 per share and sales of $17.60 billion. Fiscal 2026 projected sales are $71.07 billion-$73.16 billion, EPS of $13.19-$13.57, and adjusted EPS of $13.52-$13.90. Q1 2026 sales are expected to be $18.10 billion-$18.75 billion. Analysts lowered forecasts.
data.description: Accenture plc (NYSE: ACN) reported impressive fourth-quarter 2025 results, surpassing expectations with earnings of $3.03 per share compared to the anticipated $2.96. Sales also exceeded projections, reaching $17.60 billion against the estimated $17.36 billion. Looking ahead to fiscal 2026, Accenture anticipates sales between $71.07 billion and $73.16 billion, surpassing the consensus estimate of $69.43 billion. The company foresees a GAAP EPS for the fiscal year of $13.19 to $13.57, surpassing the analyst consensus of $12.88. Adjusted EPS is projected to be $13.52 to $13.90. For the first quarter of 2026, Accenture expects sales to be within $18.10 billion and $18.75 billion, compared to the analyst estimate of $18.451 billion. Additionally, the company plans to distribute at least $9.3 billion in cash to shareholders.
